I agree with agent1's conclusion. The nerve to the medial pterygoid muscle does not pass through the pterygomandibular space; instead, it travels more anteriorly via the masseteric space. Therefore, this makes sense given its course relative to other structures mentioned like the long buccal nerve which can be found here along with loose connective tissue from the mylohyoid region.
